name: Lead rotation — the admin's core job
next_move: Watch the Close smart view the admin works each morning. Flag leads that have been in a rep's queue > 5 days without a touch (the playbook's cadence is 5-day follow-up). Propose reassignment when a rep is over capacity or a specific rep has higher affinity for the lead's shape (Brazilian churches → André; MA venues → Eduarda; Cape Cod beach weddings → Ren; Boston corporate → Toni).
blockers: Need a canonical smart-view id from the admin to anchor the sweep
Rep capacity signal: how many open leads constitutes 'over capacity'?
Affinity heuristics need validation — current assignments above are guesses from the staff roster
live_band: The admin lives inside this view. 13,817 leads in Close, ~843 open tasks, 290 due today, 86 overdue. Rodbot's job is not to reassign silently — it's to surface drift and propose the move, then wait for a human 'yes' before the Close API call fires. Every rotation is a trust deposit to the rep receiving the lead; every silent reassignment is a withdrawal. Speed-to-lead < 5 minutes is the playbook's north star; reassignment should respect that.
notes: The playbook's 'Avoid This' section: 'Do not abandon a lead before the 5-day cadence completes.' That's the violation Rodbot watches for. Cross-reference the sales scorecard CSV for the current per-rep conversion baseline before proposing rotations — rotating a high-conversion lead to a lower-converting rep is a trust violation.

phases.title: Anchor the Close sweep
phases.goal: Produce a canonical Close smart-view target and an admin-ready shortlist schema for the rotation sweep.

deliverables.title: Canonical Close smart-view ID + filter spec

tasks.title: Pull the canonical smart-view ID used by admin for the morning sweep
Record the exact Close filters that define 'rep queue > 5 days without a touch'
Define what counts as a 'touch' (reply, call log, booking note) and encode it in the sweep spec
Confirm overdue logic and thresholds (overdue, due today, queue-age buckets) in the sweep spec
Define the exact columns needed to evaluate rotation candidates
Add fields that support rep capacity checks to the shortlist export
Add the inputs needed for affinity scoring to the shortlist export
Add baseline conversion-risk fields needed to prevent trust violations
Create the admin-facing shortlist template (copy/paste or CSV schema) from the spec

phases.title: Decide rotations safely
phases.goal: Turn the shortlist into rotation recommendations with capacity + affinity + conversion-baseline guardrails, without triggering silent changes.

tasks.title: Define 'over capacity' using a concrete open-leads threshold (or capacity metric) in Close
Validate current capacity status for each rep from Close using the chosen threshold
Write the affinity mapping rules from source/project attributes to rep candidates
Calibrate affinity rules against the last 8–12 weeks of outcomes (tasting booked / conversion)
Pull the current sales scorecard CSV to extract per-rep conversion baseline(s)
Set the rule for when a rotation is 'unsafe' (e.g., moves below baseline by more than X)
Run the shortlist through guardrails to mark candidates as 'safe recommended' vs 'hold'
Generate a one-line reason for each safe recommendation (capacity, cadence, affinity) for admin review

phases.title: Approve, then rotate (no silence)
phases.goal: Get human approval on each rotation recommendation and execute Close reassignment only after 'yes', preserving the 5-day cadence rule.

tasks.title: Assemble an admin approval list grouped by 'recommended' and 'hold (reason)'
Verify each recommended rotation still respects the 5-day cadence completion constraint
Send the approval list to the admin for explicit 'yes' per lead (or per batch with a clear checklist)
Record admin decision outcomes (yes/no) back into the activity log for auditability
Prepare the Close API payload for only 'yes' leads and only to the selected rep
Double-check that the reassignment won't conflict with a touch already made since the shortlist export
Execute the reassignment in Close for approved leads only
Verify in Close that each lead now appears under the intended rep and is updated correctly

phases.title: Watch what the rotation actually did
phases.goal: Close the loop by tracking whether reassigned leads got a touch within the cadence and whether conversion held, so next week's rotations get smarter.

tasks.title: Tag each rotated lead in Close with a rotation_batch_id for downstream tracking
Check at +24h whether the receiving rep made first contact on each rotated lead
Check at +5d whether the 5-day cadence completed on each rotated lead
Flag any rotation where the receiving rep never touched the lead — that's a trust withdrawal to surface

deliverables.title: Weekly rotation rollup for the admin

tasks.title: Aggregate the week's rotations: count, touch-rate, tasting-booked-rate, by receiving rep
Compare receiving-rep conversion on rotated leads vs their baseline to catch guardrail misses
Feed the week's outcomes back into the affinity calibration set for next week's sweep
Post the rollup to the admin with one-line 'what changed in the rules' note
